title: rosa × hibernica var. armatissima
intro: Rosa × hibernica var. armatissima, also known as the Irish briar or thorny rose, is a thorn-bearing variant of the wild rose that is native to Ireland and parts of the United Kingdom. The medicinal properties of this plant lie in its hips, which are rich in vitamins A and C, as well as flavonoids and anthocyanins, making it a valuable source of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Rosa × hibernica var. armatissima include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. Rosa × hibernica var. armatissima is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, with its hips being used to make a sweet and flavorful tea that can be enjoyed hot or cold.
